reviews.name: Rajendra Sharma
reviews.text:
I booked 4 beded  3 rooms in Chenna Guest House on dated 02.10.2025 with talked to Mr Agarwal who is owner of this hotel, as we  12 senior citizens are going on pilgrimage of religious  places of South India. As per program we are reaching Chennai in the evening of 25.10.2025. I had a very very bitter experience at the Chenna Guest House.
First as we reached reception I told  the manager of the guest house to accept the amount of money online as we have not much cash amount  but he denied it in very rudely manner. When I told him that can I request Mr Agarwal for the same his reply was very shocking,  He told me who is the Agarwal I am the manager here and Agarwal will do as I say.
We shifted in rooms and after that we went for dinner. After dinner when we went into the rooms we noticed that the blankets were not in sufficient quantity and AC's are also not working. I called the manager on the hotel phone as there was no availability of intercom facilities. I was shocked to listen to the manager's reply he told me 'All AC's are working and sufficient blankets are there don't disturb me again if you have any problem come down and don't call me. I sent my relative to collect blankets but he did not support He said in six blankets 12 persons can adjust, when we told him that there we are ladies and gents of different relationship and not possible to adjust in one blanket, his reply was very surprising he said all gents and ladies relative can sleep together, when we oppose his statement he came in front of reception and started men handling. He didn't stop here and he threatened us that his relative is the minister and he has an approach in the police and can call the police and arrest us all.
We were afraid and took the decision to leave the hotel. I called the owner of the hotel Mr Agarwal but he also supported his manager. When we requested him to  refund the amount given by me, he denied my request. At last we all senior citizens left the hotel at midnight and shifted to another hotel.
My request to all please think hundreds of times before booking this hotel. If you want safety and your security then don't book this hotel.

reviewContext.Rooms: Rooms was very dirty and stinky

reviews.name: NEETA SHARMA
reviews.text:
Chenna guest house is bad property its location is worst..Staff member's attitude is very bad towards customers.  Even they didn't supply sufficient blankets, abd don't care about customers complaint. Manager is very rough in nature and very quarrelsome,  When my husband told him that please send blankets in room and to send someone to check AC that was not working.  He told that he haven't any person if you want something you comedown,  when my husband went to collect blanket he started manhandling and in worst situation we senior citizen left hotel in mid night and shifted in other one.
I personally request to all not to book this guest house.

title: Hotel Thaj Regency
subTitle: ஹோட்டல் தாஜ் ரீஜெனசி
description: Functional rooms, some with air-conditioning, in a straightforward budget hotel.
address: 300, Triplicane High Rd, Padupakkam, Triplicane, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600005, India
hotelDescription:
Set on a commercial street among shops and informal eateries, this no-nonsense budget hotel is a 16-minute walk from Chepauk train station and 20 minutes' walk from Marina Beach.
 The basic rooms come with flat-screen TVs and ceiling fans. Upgraded rooms add air-conditioning. There are also family units. Room service is available 24/7. Kids age 9 and under stay free with an adult.
 Parking is offered.

reviews.name: Shehzil Khan
reviews.text: Very good

reviews.name: Shreyash Deokar
reviews.text:
This was by far the worst hotel experience I have ever had. Please do not book this hotel even by mistake.

From the moment we arrived, the staff behaved in an extremely rude and unprofessional manner. They were not helpful at all and showed no interest in resolving even basic issues. It felt like guests were treated as a burden rather than customers.

The rooms were in terrible condition. There was a strong, unpleasant smell throughout the room. Cleanliness was clearly not maintained. Basic facilities were missing — no proper chairs, no table, no drinking water provided, and poor air ventilation. The room felt suffocating and uncomfortable. There was no sense of safety or security.

For the amount of money being charged, the service provided was absolutely unacceptable. It honestly felt like a scam. There was zero comfort, zero hospitality, and zero accountability.

If the owner happens to read this review, please understand that customer satisfaction is not optional in the hospitality industry. Train your staff properly, improve room maintenance, ensure cleanliness, and provide basic amenities. Charging money without providing even minimum standards of service will eventually damage your reputation permanently.

I strongly do not recommend this hotel to anyone. Save your money and choose a place that respects its guests.

reviews.text: This is one of the worst hotels I have ever visited. The receptionist was very rude, and the cleanliness was extremely poor. The geyser was not working, and the AC made a lot of noise. They provide only basic rooms with very limited facilities. They even provide drinking water. I would not recommend this hotel.

reviews.name: HARI OM PANDEY
reviews.text:
would like to provide honest feedback regarding my recent stay at your hotel.
Unfortunately, the experience was far below expectations. The hotel staff was not friendly or welcoming toward customers. Hospitality plays a major role in customer satisfaction, and the lack of courteous behavior directly affects the hotel’s reputation and revenue.
The rooms are extremely small and poorly planned. There is no proper seating arrangement and no adequate facility for hanging clothes. When using the bathroom, guests are forced to leave their clothes on the bed, and after bathing, they must again pick up their clothes from the bed to change. This is highly inconvenient and uncomfortable.
Additionally, the presence of cockroaches in the room is completely unacceptable and raises serious concerns about cleanliness and hygiene.
Furthermore, the television and internet services do not function properly, causing further inconvenience during the stay.
I strongly recommend immediate improvements in staff behavior, room planning, cleanliness standards, and technical services. Enhancing these aspects is essential for customer satisfaction, positive reviews, and ultimately for protecting and increasing the hotel’s revenue.
I hope the management will take corrective action at the earliest.

description: Simply furnished rooms in a straightforward hotel offering complimentary Wi-Fi & parking.
address: : 407, Anna Salai, near Kalaignar Tv, Subbarayan Nagar, Teynampet, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600018, India
hotelDescription:
This modest hotel off a main thoroughfare is 15 km from Chennai International Airport, 5 km from Gandhi Beach along the Bay of Bengal, and 2 km from the Semmozhi Poonga botanical gardens.
 Simply furnished rooms feature cable TV, Wi-Fi access, and tea and coffeemaking facilities. Upgraded rooms add flat-screens and air-conditioning. Room service is available 24/7.
 Parking is complimentary.

similarHotelsNearby.name: GT Grande Hotel-Near US Consulate & Singapore Consulate
OYO RSM Shelters Near Shri Ayyappan Temple

ownerUpdates.text: Thangam Balaji Guest House is a budget guest house located in the heart of Chennai city. We are close to VFS Global Visa Application Centre, the US Consulate, Shankar Nethralaya Eye Hospital, Kauvery Hospital, Apollo Cancer Hospital, and the Metro Railway Station. Our guest house is ideal for business travelers, families, and medical visitors looking for affordable rooms with a homely atmosphere. Please note, parking is not available at our property. Whether for a visa appointment, medical treatment, or a short city stay, Thangam Balaji Guest House offers comfort, cleanliness, and convenience in central Chennai.

reviews.name: Anandjr 910
reviews.text:
The manager and the owner were extremely kind, friendly, and helpful throughout my stay. The room was neat, clean, and well maintained.
The location is good and easily accessible within Chennai. The price is very reasonable, making it one of the best guest houses in Chennai for a low budget. This place is highly suitable for solo travelers, business travelers, and families as well.
I would definitely recommend this guest house to anyone looking for a comfortable and affordable stay in Chennai.

title: Metro Park
subTitle: மெட்ரோ பார்க்
description: Straightforward rooms with air conditioning in a modest hotel offering a restaurant & breakfast.
address: 61/8, Karumari Amman Koil St, Thirunagar, Bhakthavathchalam Colony, Vadapalani, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600026, India
hotelDescription:
On a side street in a commercial area, this relaxed hotel with a glass facade is a 7-minute walk from both Arulmigu Vadapalani Murugan Temple and Vadpalani metro station. It's 13 minutes away on foot from Nexus Vijaya Mall.
 Simply furnished rooms come with air conditioning and flat-screen TVs, as well as en suite wet rooms.
 There's a restaurant. Breakfast is available.

A 13-minute walk from Chennai Suburban Railway Station, this humble hotel along a bustling urban street is 5 km from Marina Beach and 8 km from the Kapaleeshwarar Temple.
 The straightforward rooms are simply furnished and come with satellite TV and ceiling fans; some provide sitting areas. There are also family rooms. Room service is available 24/7.
 Parking is offered.

title: Hotel White Park
subTitle: ஹோட்டல் ஒயிட் பார்க்
description: Simply furnished rooms with sitting areas in a relaxed hotel offering breakfast & Wi-Fi.
address: 14, Kumarappa St, Periamet, Park Town, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600003, India
hotelDescription:
Set in a bustling area, this relaxed hotel is an 11-minute walk from Puratchi Thalaivar Dr. M.G. Ramachandran Central Railway Station and 2 km from the Government Museum.
 Simply furnished rooms feature Wi-Fi and flat-screen TVs, as well as sitting areas. Room service is available. Kids age 9 and under stay at no extra cost with a parent.
 Continental breakfast is available.

similarHotelsNearby.name: Hotel White Mount, Rama Pillai Street, Periyamet, Chennai, Tamil Nadu

reviews.name: Ramachandra 131
reviews.text: The price is to high for which the rooms can be get at some better rooms with same price and it is suitated in small streets.

reviews.name: DEEPAK KUMAR
reviews.text:
This place should honestly not be called a hotel. My stay at Hotel White Park, Chennai was a complete nightmare.
There was NO WATER in the taps when I checked in. I informed the staff immediately, and shockingly, the problem was not fixed even after 1 hour. Imagine paying for a room where you can’t even wash your hands or use the toilet properly. Absolutely unacceptable.
The staff was rude and completely uncooperative. Instead of taking responsibility or apologizing, they acted like this kind of service failure is normal. Zero professionalism.
There was no intercom phone in the room, so every time I needed help, I had to physically go down and chase the staff. In 2026, this is beyond ridiculous.
On top of all this, no complimentary water bottles were provided. Not even basic hospitality standards were met.
This hotel is a disgrace to the hospitality industry. I strongly warn everyone: DO NOT STAY HERE unless you enjoy suffering, wasting money, and being treated like your comfort doesn’t matter.

reviews.name: rohit kumar
reviews.text: Pathetic.

*Not recommended at all to stay here*

title: Hotel Chandra Park
subTitle: ஹோட்டல் சந்திரா பார்க்
description: Simple rooms in a laid-back property offering a restaurant & a bar, plus free Wi-Fi & parking.
address: 9, Gandhi Irwin Rd, Egmore, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600008, India
hotelDescription:
Across from Chennai Egmore train station, this casual hotel is a 14-minute walk from the archaeological collection at the Government Museum and 5 km from Marina Beach.
 Straightforward rooms with wood furnishings offer free Wi-Fi, flat-screen TVs and desks. Upgraded rooms add sitting areas with sofas. Room service is available.
 Parking is complimentary. There's a laid-back restaurant, a cafe and a bar, plus conference facilities.

title: Hotel Singapore
subTitle: ஹோட்டல் சிங்கப்பூர்
description: Unpretentious rooms in a casual budget hotel with an informal international restaurant & free Wi-Fi.
address: 20, Kennet Ln, Egmore, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600008, India
hotelDescription:
A 4-minute walk from Chennai Egmore train station, this casual budget hotel is a kilometer from the Government Museum and 4 km from Arulmigu Sri Parthasarathyswamy, an ornate 8th-century Hindu temple.
 The unpretentious rooms come with free Wi-Fi, flat-screen TV and safes, plus tea and coffeemaking facilities. Upgraded rooms add air conditioning. Room service is offered.
 There's an informal international restaurant. Parking is available.

Old building hence rooms looks old, furniture should  be upgraded.

Lot of old stuff like mattresses,  cots, beds lie all over the aisle  way , giving bad look.
Few Lifts  fail to operate,  needs proper Maintenance.
For those, in need of a room, nearer to Railway  stations,  thus is the  place.

reviews.name: Ramamoorthy Renganathan
reviews.text:
In a lane opp Egmore Chennai Railway station. Walkable from Station.
Spacious rooms. Moderate rent. 24 hr chk in check out. No parking space inside.
Hot water for bath has to be brought in bucket to room for bath by room service. No hot water available on tap in room.
Excellent room service.
Accepts cash and UPI pmt.
No restaurant inside.
Next door is Saravana Bhavan vegetarian restaurant + self service) . Good food and coffee. Moderately priced food.
Walkable to railway station bus stop and shops

title: Hotel Adithya
subTitle: ஹோட்டல் ஆதித்யா
description: Unpretentious rooms, some with air-conditioning, in a modest hotel offering a casual restaurant.
address: View Larger Map, 24, Naval Hospital Rd, Periamet, Park Town, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600003, India
street: View Larger Map, 24, Naval Hospital Rd
hotelDescription:
Amid commercial buildings in the bustling city centre, this modest hotel is a 15-minute walk from Chennai Moore Market Complex train station and 4 km from the circa-1644 British-built Fort St. George.
 Unassuming rooms provide Wi-Fi, flat-screen TVs, ceiling fans and minifridges, as well as safes. Some feature sitting areas, while upgraded rooms add air-conditioning. Room service is offered 24/7.
 There's a casual restaurant. Secured parking is available.
